registry.description: Iniquity is the deliberate turning or twisting away from what is right — not accidental wrongdoing but the wilful perversion of the moral order. The primary Hebrew root (avon) carries the image of something bent or twisted out of its proper shape. Iniquity is cumulative: it builds up, is passed down through generations, and creates a kind of moral debt that requires addressing. The word is closely associated with guilt and punishment, because iniquity carries consequences by its nature.

1a) iniquity

1c) consequence of or punishment for iniquity
